title: Fix day-zero CLI create and login smoke bugs
description: Manual smoke testing found two blocking day-zero issues: forge create sends nil optional array fields to non-null ticket columns when flags are omitted, and the login/web flow emits htmx 400 errors around /login and /tickets. Fix the root causes, update README with reliable local smoke-test instructions, and verify the artifact/search/analytics flow from a fresh local DB.
close_reason: Fixed day-zero create/login/CLI smoke bugs, updated README smoke instructions, and verified fresh DB CLI/web/artifact/search/analytics flow.

title: Review hardening: priority ordering and decomposition validation
description: Repo review found current-state hardening work before continuing Phase 2: claim-next should honor documented priority semantics, decomposition should reject child dependency cycles, and AGENTS.md should record the new branch-per-slice workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Claim-next orders priority 0 before larger numbers; decomposition rejects cyclic child dependencies before writing children; AGENTS.md documents branch-per-slice commit/push workflow; go test ./... and go vet ./... pass.
close_reason: Fixed claim priority ordering and matching index, added decomposition cycle validation, documented branch-per-slice workflow, pinned Go to 1.26.3, and verified tests/vet/race/govulncheck.

title: Wire live Postgres runtime into API and CLI surfaces
description: p1-12 needs the CLI to execute real Phase 1 operations with predictable JSON output. The current code has sqlc stores and services plus Huma route/OpenAPI registration, but no shared runtime composition that opens pgx, constructs db.Queries/services, and lets API or CLI commands call live operations. Add this integration layer before claiming the CLI task as functional.
close_reason: Added live runtime composition over pgx/sqlc/services/jobs plus API and CLI dependency seams for shared runtime use.

title: Add Phase 1 correctness test suite
description: Add concurrency, crash recovery, idempotency, blocked-work, and state-machine tests for the execution core.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove no duplicate claims, expired leases return eligible work, blocked tickets are not claimed until resolved, terminal attempts reject invalid transitions, and claim idempotency replays correctly.
close_reason: Added Phase 1 correctness regression suite covering duplicate-claim prevention, eligibility filters, expiry/block behavior, terminal transition guards, and claim idempotency persistence.

title: Build JSON-first Phase 1 CLI
description: Build core CLI commands over the same domain/API semantics.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports create, propose, claim-next, heartbeat, checkpoint, complete, fail, block, list, get; --json output is stable; commands have predictable exit codes.
close_reason: Implemented JSON-first CLI commands over the shared runtime for create/propose/claim/list/get/heartbeat/checkpoint/complete/fail/block/cancel with stable JSON output and tests.

title: Expose Phase 1 Huma REST API
description: Expose core execution operations through REST using Huma and OpenAPI generation.
acceptance_criteria: REST routes exist for tickets, propose, ready, claim-next, attempts, heartbeat, checkpoint, complete, fail, block, cancel, events, and basic artifact registration; OpenAPI is generated.
close_reason: Registered Phase 1 Huma REST route surface and OpenAPI generation under /api/v1 with tests covering required paths.

title: Add River workers for lease expiry and cleanup
description: Add Postgres-backed background workers for stale lease expiry and idempotency cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: worker expires stale running attempts, updates tickets according to retry policy, writes events, and cleans expired idempotency records.
close_reason: Added River-ready maintenance worker for lease expiry and idempotency cleanup, with sqlc queries, River job wrapper, and tests.

title: Implement idempotency middleware and claim replay
description: Implement machine-facing idempotency support, with special handling for claim-next response replay after committed claims.
acceptance_criteria: same key and request hash replays stored response; same key with different request conflicts; claim-next stores ticket_id/attempt_id/context response in the claim transaction.
close_reason: Implemented claim-next idempotency replay/conflict handling, request hashing, and in-statement claim response storage.

title: Implement attempt completion, failure, block, cancel, and expiry transitions
description: Implement explicit ticket and attempt state machine transitions for terminal and blocked outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: complete/fail/block/cancel/expire enforce valid transitions; blocked work is distinct from failure; ticket statuses update per retry/review/block policy; events are written transactionally.
close_reason: Implemented terminal attempt transitions for complete/fail/block/cancel/expire with ticket status updates, event writes, service validation, and tests.

title: Implement transactional claim-next with context bundle
description: Implement Postgres-owned claim-next semantics with eligibility filtering and a claim context bundle returned to the caller.
acceptance_criteria: claim-next filters by workspace/project/status/type/tags/harness/capabilities/dependencies/retry eligibility; creates attempt and claimed event in one transaction; returns ticket, attempt, and context bundle.
close_reason: Implemented transactional claim-next SQL, generated query methods, claim service validation, and claim context bundle hydration with tests.

title: Implement attempt heartbeat and checkpoint
description: Implement cheap heartbeats for liveness and durable checkpoints for resumable progress.
acceptance_criteria: heartbeat extends lease and writes heartbeat event; checkpoint updates attempt summary/next step/progress, creates checkpoint row, and writes checkpointed event.
close_reason: Implemented heartbeat and checkpoint SQL mutations with event writes, service validation, and focused tests.

title: Implement ticket creation, proposal, validation, and listing
description: Implement domain/service behavior for creating tickets, proposing backlog tickets, validating ticket quality, and listing tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Human-created and agent-created tickets are supported; agent-created tickets default to backlog unless enqueue is permitted; validation checks title, project, type, acceptance criteria, and useful context.
close_reason: Implemented TicketService for create, propose, validation, dependency/event recording, and listing defaults with tests.

title: Generate sqlc models and core queries
description: Add sqlc configuration, handwritten SQL queries, and generated Go types for core Phase 1 tables.
acceptance_criteria: sqlc generation succeeds; queries cover create/list/get tickets, dependencies, attempts, checkpoints, events, idempotency, and metrics basics.
close_reason: Completed sqlc configuration, handwritten Phase 1 core queries, generated pgx-backed models/query methods, dependency setup, and compile-time query surface tests.

title: Create core Postgres migrations
description: Add migrations for workspaces, projects, tickets, ticket dependencies, attempts, attempt checkpoints, events, idempotency keys, API keys, and attempt metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Migrations apply cleanly to empty Postgres; schema represents PRD Phase 1 fields; rollback strategy is documented or intentionally omitted for initial development.
close_reason: Completed initial Postgres schema migration with core Forge tables, claim-path indexes, Goose-compatible Up/Down sections, tests, and disposable Postgres apply verification.

title: Add configuration and process boot
description: Add configuration loading and process startup plumbing for API server, worker process, and CLI commands.
acceptance_criteria: Configuration supports env/config file defaults; server and worker can start with clear validation errors; tests cover config parsing.
close_reason: Completed config loading and process boot plumbing with defaults, JSON config file support, env overrides, validation, CLI startup paths, and tests.

title: Bootstrap Go monolith skeleton
description: Create the initial Forge Go project structure and bootable binary shape for server, worker, mcp, tui, and CLI command modes.
acceptance_criteria: go.mod exists; cmd/forge/main.go boots; internal package layout matches PRD; forge --help works; no product behavior is implemented beyond command skeletons.
close_reason: Completed Go monolith skeleton with command help, package layout, tests, and build verification.

title: Phase 1: Execution Core
description: Build the correctness-first Forge execution ledger: schema, transactional claim-next, attempts, events, checkpoints, idempotency, REST, CLI, workers, and verification.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 supports the day-zero flow from PRD.md: create ticket, claim with context, heartbeat/checkpoint, complete/fail/block, write events, replay claim idempotency, and prove concurrency/lease correctness.
close_reason: All 15 Phase 1 execution-core tasks are closed and go test ./... passes.

title: Implement Phase 4 full-text search slice
description: Add the first Phase 4 search slice: Postgres full-text search across ticket title/description, attempt summary/output, event data, and artifact names, exposed through a minimal human-ops API or web surface.
acceptance_criteria: Postgres full-text search covers ticket titles/descriptions, attempt summaries/output, event data, and artifact names; a runtime-backed API or web page exposes search results in the current human-ops style; focused tests and go test ./... pass.
close_reason: Implemented Phase 4 Postgres full-text search slice with /search web surface; verified with focused packages and go test ./...

title: Phase 4 analytics metrics slice
description: Wire attempt metrics into CLI/runtime submissions so Codex and other harnesses can report token, cost, duration, and retry data, then expose basic analytics summaries by model and harness with tests and minimal human-facing output.
acceptance_criteria: Attempts can accept metrics from CLI/runtime harness flows; analytics summary/by-model/by-harness are available through runtime/API/CLI surfaces; tests cover persistence and grouping behavior.
close_reason: Implemented attempt metrics ingestion and basic analytics summary/by-model/by-harness across runtime, CLI, REST, and MCP; verified with go test ./...

title: Implement local proof artifact storage
description: First concrete Phase 4 slice: local proof artifacts registered by forge codex complete/block should be stored locally, registered with stable local://artifacts/... URLs, retrievable, and openable from the human /artifacts/{id} route.
acceptance_criteria: forge codex complete/block local proof paths produce registered artifacts; local artifact content can be retrieved by id; /artifacts/{id} renders a usable open/download surface instead of hidden links; focused tests cover the behavior.
close_reason: Implemented local artifact storage, Codex proof uploads, /artifacts/{id} retrieval, web links, docs, and tests.

title: P3-10 Add project and workspace admin screens
description: Add minimal project and workspace administration screens for human operators. Keep them focused on setup and inspection, not project-management dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: Human web UI exposes minimal workspace and project admin screens; common setup data is visible and editable where appropriate; tests cover routes and validation; screens avoid custom-field sprawl and board-management workflows.
close_reason: Implemented minimal human workspace/project admin screens: /workspaces lists and creates workspaces, /workspaces/{id} inspects projects and creates project scopes, server routes stay auth-protected, docs cover setup path, and tests verify routes avoid dashboard/board-management scope creep.

title: P3-09 Add human auth flow
description: Add a simple admin token or session auth flow for human-facing web views. Keep the first version boring, explicit, and compatible with the Go monolith.
acceptance_criteria: Human web routes are protected by a documented auth mechanism; local development remains straightforward; tests cover unauthenticated and authenticated access; secrets are not logged or committed.
close_reason: Implemented human web auth: server config requires admin_token, /login issues an opaque HttpOnly session cookie, human web routes redirect unauthenticated browsers, bearer/header token access supports scripted checks, README documents setup, and go test ./... plus go build ./cmd/forge pass.

title: P3-08 Add shareable deep links
description: Add copyable and shareable deep links for tickets, attempts, artifacts, and proposed follow-ups across the web UI and TUI command surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Stable URL patterns exist for ticket, attempt, artifact, and proposed follow-up inspection; UI surfaces expose copyable IDs or links; docs explain link semantics; tests cover route generation.
close_reason: Implemented shareable deep links for ticket, attempt, artifact, and proposed follow-up inspection routes; web pages render copyable route links; TUI queue/detail surfaces print matching links; README documents link semantics; go test ./... and go build ./cmd/forge pass.

title: P3-07 Add server-rendered ticket list and detail pages
description: Add the first templ + htmx web inspection surfaces: ticket list and ticket detail. Keep web UI server-rendered, fast, and useful for links shared in chats, PRs, and handoffs.
acceptance_criteria: Web ticket list and detail pages render from Go handlers; pages have stable routes and useful empty/error states; tests cover handler/component output; styling is polished enough for shared inspection without becoming a SPA.
close_reason: Implemented server-rendered ticket list and detail pages, mounted them under forge server, added runtime-backed HTML tests for list/detail/empty/error states, and updated README server docs.

title: P3-06 Add proposed work triage flow
description: Build a lightweight review inbox for agent-created proposed tickets. Support ready, refine, merge, reject, archive, or enqueue decisions without backlog grooming ceremony.
acceptance_criteria: Proposed-ticket triage has shared service operations and UI-ready data shapes; tests cover approval/rejection paths and source attribution; documentation explains when agents should propose versus enqueue.
close_reason: Implemented service/runtime proposed-work triage operations with UI-ready source attribution, dedicated proposed-ticket query, tests for list/approval/refine/reject flows, and documentation for propose versus enqueue.

title: P3-05 Add human operation transitions
description: Add review, ready, reopen, unblock, and archive operations for human operators. Keep the operations low-ceremony and backed by runtime services/events rather than UI-only state changes.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime/service operations exist for review, ready, reopen, unblock, and archive where needed; state changes write events; tests cover allowed and rejected transitions; TUI/web callers can share the same operations.
close_reason: Implemented runtime-backed human ticket transitions for ready, reopen, unblock, request review, review approve/reject, and archive; state changes write ticket events and tests cover allowed/rejected transitions. Verified go test ./..., go build ./cmd/forge, and git diff --check.

title: P3-04 Add TUI attempt and event timelines
description: Add TUI timelines for attempts, checkpoints, blockers, events, and proof artifacts so developers can understand what happened on a ticket without reading raw database rows.
acceptance_criteria: Ticket detail includes attempt, checkpoint, blocker, event, and artifact timeline sections; tests cover ordering and empty states; terminal and blocked states are visually distinct without noisy decoration.
close_reason: Implemented TUI attempts/checkpoints/events/artifacts timelines with blocked/terminal state rendering; verified with go test ./..., go build ./cmd/forge, and git diff --check.

title: P3-03 Build TUI ticket detail view
description: Add the TUI ticket detail surface for one selected ticket, focused on claim context, acceptance criteria, verification commands, paths, current attempt state, and copyable IDs or commands.
acceptance_criteria: Queue selection can open a ticket detail view; detail content is dense but readable; tests cover view model construction and navigation; the view avoids board-management interactions.
close_reason: Implemented the TUI ticket detail surface, wired queue selection to load attempts through the shared runtime query path, added deterministic detail/navigation/error tests, and verified go test ./..., go build, and git diff --check.

title: P3-02 Build TUI queue view foundation
description: Build the first Bubble Tea TUI surface for inspecting Forge work queues. Start with a fast read-only queue console that can list and filter tickets by status, type, project, harness, and agent where data is available.
acceptance_criteria: forge tui opens a keyboard-first queue view; the view has deterministic test coverage for state/model updates; it uses shared runtime/query surfaces rather than parallel business logic; empty and error states are visible and calm.
close_reason: Implemented the Bubble Tea queue foundation, wired forge tui through the shared runtime ListTickets path, added deterministic model/CLI coverage, and verified go test ./... plus forge tui --help.

title: P3-01 Define human operations UX contract
description: Define the Phase 3 human-operations contract for Forge: TUI-first, polished web later, dense calm inspection surfaces, keyboard-friendly navigation, proof-first timelines, and no Jira-style ceremony. Use the existing PRD and UX mockups to lock the view order and implementation boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Document names the Phase 3 UX principles, initial TUI and web view order, core interactions, non-goals, and verification expectations; links to existing UX mockups; future TUI/web implementation tickets can reference it without re-litigating product direction.
close_reason: Defined the Phase 3 human-operations UX contract, view order, non-goals, verification expectations, and Beads breakdown in docs/human-operations.md; verified dependency graph has no cycles and go test ./... passes.

title: P2-09 Add REST CLI MCP parity tests and Phase 2 closeout docs
description: Add parity coverage proving REST, CLI, and MCP expose equivalent semantics for the core agent operations. Finish Phase 2 with concise operational documentation for developers and agents.
acceptance_criteria: Parity tests cover create/propose, claim, checkpoint, update, complete, fail/block, get/list, attach, decompose, and capability registration where implemented; Phase 2 docs explain what is testable and how agents should use Forge.
close_reason: Added Phase 2 REST/CLI/MCP parity coverage, recorded update-ticket REST binding in the shared contract, documented the closeout adapter matrix, and verified go test ./... passes.

title: P2-08 Add harness integration examples
description: Document and example real harness integration for Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and custom agent systems. Include minimal flows for claiming work, reporting progress, creating tickets, and attaching proof.
acceptance_criteria: Harness examples are runnable or copy-pasteable; Codex path is tested against the implemented convenience commands; documentation emphasizes low-friction agent workflows and avoids Jira-style ceremony.
close_reason: Added harness integration documentation for Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and custom agents, linked it from docs, refreshed MCP lifecycle wording, and added CLI coverage for documented Codex convenience commands.

title: P2-07 Add Codex convenience commands
description: Add thin Codex-oriented commands for claim, checkpoint, complete with proof artifacts, propose follow-up, and blocked-attempt capture. Keep them adapter-like over the core runtime, optimized for fast terminal use inside a harness.
acceptance_criteria: Commands exist with JSON-first output; commands reuse shared runtime operations; proof artifacts and follow-up proposals are easy to pass from Codex sessions; CLI tests cover command behavior and error cases.
close_reason: Added JSON-first Codex convenience commands for claim, checkpoint, complete with proof artifacts, follow-up ticket creation, and blocked-attempt capture using shared runtime operations.

title: P2-06 Expose MCP tools over runtime services
description: Implement the MCP tool surface for create, propose, create-from-attempt, claim, heartbeat, checkpoint, update, complete, fail, block, list, get, attach, decompose, and register-capabilities using the shared runtime services and schemas.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tools call the same domain operations as REST and CLI; JSON Schema descriptions are exposed for each tool; integration tests cover representative happy paths and validation failures.
close_reason: Implemented MCP runtime tool dispatch for Phase 2 operations, including update-ticket service support, schema alignment, and coverage for happy paths plus validation failures.

title: P2-05 Add MCP server mode and lifecycle
description: Add an MCP server entrypoint that boots Forge with the same runtime dependencies as the API and CLI. Keep the MCP layer thin so it delegates to domain services instead of creating a parallel implementation.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server mode starts locally; lifecycle and error handling are documented; startup tests verify the server can register tools from shared schema definitions.
close_reason: Added MCP server lifecycle registry, forge mcp runtime boot path, contract-backed tool registration tests, CLI startup test, and lifecycle docs.

title: P2-04 Add planner decomposition flow
description: Add an explicit decomposition flow for planner agents and human-guided planning. Support taking a larger ticket or phase and producing proposed child tasks with dependencies, acceptance criteria, and proof expectations before execution starts.
acceptance_criteria: Decomposition can be requested through shared runtime services; generated child task proposals preserve parent/blocked relationships; tests cover manual input and agent-provided decomposition payloads.
close_reason: Added planner decomposition service flow for proposed or claimable child tasks, dependency wiring, parent/root preservation, proof expectations, runtime wiring, and tests.

title: P2-03 Add ticket templates and create-from-attempt validation
description: Implement low-friction templates for agent-created work: bug, feature, documentation, review, investigation, cleanup, and follow-up. Include validation that helps agents create useful tickets without turning Forge into a high-ceremony tracker.
acceptance_criteria: Template definitions exist with required and optional fields; create-from-attempt can produce a validated proposed or open ticket from failed work evidence; validation messages are concise and agent-actionable.
close_reason: Added low-friction ticket templates, create-from-attempt service flow, validation for agent-created follow-up work, expanded ticket types, and tests.

title: P2-02 Add agent capability registration and lookup
description: Add durable records for agent and harness capabilities, including tool support, transport support, artifact abilities, preferred claim behavior, and optional metadata that helps Forge shape context bundles for a worker.
acceptance_criteria: Capabilities can be registered, updated, listed, and looked up through shared runtime services; validation rejects malformed capability declarations; tests cover at least one Codex-style registration.
close_reason: Added durable agent capability registration, lookup/list service, richer schema fields, sqlc queries, runtime wiring, and Codex-style validation tests.

title: P2-01 Define agent integration contracts and JSON schemas
description: Define the Phase 2 contract surface for agent-facing operations across REST, CLI, and MCP. Establish stable JSON Schema payloads and response shapes for create, propose, create-from-attempt, claim, heartbeat, checkpoint, update, complete, fail, block, list, get, attach, decompose, and capability registration.
acceptance_criteria: Schema package exists with tests; schemas are documented for LLM/tool use; REST, CLI, and MCP implementations can share the same contract definitions without hand-written drift.
close_reason: Added shared agent operation contract package with JSON schemas, tests, REST operation bindings, and docs.

title: Document Phase 1 local development and day-zero flow
description: Document setup, migrations, server/worker/CLI usage, and the PRD day-zero flow for developers and future agents.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs explain how to run Postgres, apply migrations, start services, run CLI commands, execute tests, and walk through create->claim->checkpoint->complete/fail/block.
close_reason: Added README with Phase 1 setup, migration, runtime command, JSON CLI, day-zero flow, and correctness test documentation.

title: Add proof artifact registration for Phase 1
description: Add lightweight artifact metadata registration for diffs, test output, final responses, diagnostics, and handoff evidence without full storage abstraction yet.
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts can be registered against tickets/attempts with type and role; claim context and detail views can retrieve registered proof metadata.
close_reason: Implemented lightweight artifact metadata registration/retrieval, runtime wiring, and forge attach JSON command with tests.

title: Phase 3: Human Operations
description: Build fast, beautiful, low-friction TUI and web surfaces for developers to inspect, steer, and trust agent work.
acceptance_criteria: Developers can inspect queues, tickets, attempts, checkpoints, blockers, artifacts, proposed work, and execution activity through polished TUI and web views.
close_reason: Phase 3 Human Operations is complete: TUI queue/detail/timelines, human operations, proposed-work triage, server-rendered web inspection, auth, shareable deep links, and minimal workspace/project admin screens are implemented; all 10 child tasks are closed and go test ./... plus go build ./cmd/forge pass.

title: Phase 2: Agent Integration
description: Make Forge easy for real agents and harnesses to use through MCP, capabilities, templates, Codex convenience commands, and parity tests.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use REST, CLI, or MCP with equivalent semantics; Codex convenience commands exist; agent-created work follows templates and validation.
close_reason: Phase 2 Agent Integration is complete: contracts, capabilities, templates, decomposition, MCP runtime tools, Codex convenience commands, harness examples, parity coverage, and closeout docs are implemented and go test ./... passes.

title: Phase 4: Artifacts, Search, and Analytics
description: Make Forge useful as execution history through artifact storage, full-text search, metrics, summaries, and webhooks.
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts, search, and analytics expose proof and historical execution context across projects, agents, harnesses, and attempts.

title: Phase 5: Intelligence and Advanced Coordination
description: Add semantic search, historical similarity, recommendations, policy, advanced RBAC, and optional realtime/observability integrations after real usage exists.
acceptance_criteria: Advanced coordination features are added only after the core ledger has real usage data and remains low-ceremony.
